name: J1: First Compile
description: User wraps model with torch.compile() and hits first errors, crashes, or isn't sure they did it right
question: How do I get torch.compile to work on my model?

name: J2: Performance Diagnosis
description: Compiled model is slower than eager or speedup is disappointing — diagnostic workflow routes to root cause
question: My compiled model is slower than eager — or the speedup is disappointing.

name: J3: Correctness & Debugging
description: Compiled model gives different results than eager — numerical mismatches, NaN/Inf, regressions
question: The compiled model gives different results than eager.

name: J4: Graph Breaks
description: Parts of model fall back to eager unexpectedly, causing slowdowns — excessive breaks fragment compiled regions
question: Parts of my model fall back to eager — or it's unexpectedly slow and I don't know why.

name: J5: Dynamic Shapes
description: Model recompiles every time input shape changes — recompilation storms from shape guard failures
question: My model recompiles every time the input shape changes.

name: J6: Compile-Time Performance
description: Compilation itself is the bottleneck — blocks iteration in dev, causes cold-start in production
question: Compilation takes minutes and my GPU sits idle.

name: J7: Performance Optimization
description: torch.compile gives speedup but user wants more — fusion, codegen tuning, custom Triton, memory optimization
question: torch.compile gives me a speedup, but I want more.

name: J8: Custom & Higher-Order Ops
description: Custom operators or advanced patterns (vmap, checkpoint, torch.cond, flex attention) don't work with compile
question: My custom operator or advanced pattern doesn't work with torch.compile.

name: J9: Export & Serialization
description: User wants to save compiled model for deployment — torch.export, ExportedProgram, AOTInductor
question: I want to save my compiled model so I can load it elsewhere.
note: TODO: not yet fully mapped in ontology relationships
